title: Bat Removal & Exclusion Services in University Heights, Bronx | $900-$2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in University Heights, Bronx. Humane, effective solutions for homes and buildings. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: University Heights residents facing bat infestations need fast, humane, and effective solutions to protect their homes and health. Our professional bat removal and exclusion services provide comprehensive inspections, safe bat removal, and permanent exclusion methods to prevent future entry. We serve all of University Heights, from historic brownstones to modern apartment complexes near Bronx Community College.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC brings over 15 years of bat removal expertise to University Heights, using humane exclusion techniques that comply with all New York wildlife protection laws. Our licensed technicians understand the unique architecture of University Heights buildings and provide guaranteed solutions with follow-up inspections to ensure your property remains bat-free.
localContext: University Heights' mix of pre-war apartment buildings, single-family homes near Hall of Fame Terrace, and structures surrounding the historic Gould Memorial Library often have older rooflines, chimneys, and ventilation systems that provide ideal entry points for bats. The tree-lined streets and proximity to parks make this neighborhood particularly attractive to bat colonies seeking urban roosting sites.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my University Heights home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include hearing scratching or squeaking sounds at dusk or dawn, finding small dark droppings (guano) near entry points or in attics, spotting bats flying around your roofline at sunset, or noticing brown staining around potential entry holes. Bats often enter through gaps as small as 3/8 of an inch in older University Heights buildings.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in New York, and what is the process?
faqs.answer: Yes, bat removal is legal in New York when performed by licensed professionals using humane exclusion methods. We cannot remove bats during maternity season (May-August) when flightless young are present. Our process includes thorough inspection, identification of entry points, installation of one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not return, sealing all entry points, cleanup of guano, and follow-up inspection.

faqs.question: What does the $900-$2,500 price range depend on?
faqs.answer: Pricing varies based on the size of the bat colony, number of entry points, accessibility of roosting areas, extent of exclusion work needed, and amount of guano cleanup required. A simple single-entry point exclusion on a accessible area may cost around $900, while a large colony in a multi-story University Heights building with multiple entry points and extensive sealing work would be closer to $2,500. We provide detailed estimates after inspection.

faqs.question: Are bats dangerous, and should I be concerned about diseases?
faqs.answer: While bats are beneficial for controlling insects, they can carry rabies and their guano can contain histoplasmosis spores that are harmful if inhaled. Bats should never be handled directly. Additionally, bat infestations can cause property damage, unpleasant odors, and attract other pests. Professional removal ensures your safety and prevents health risks to your University Heights household.
